description: Documented self-install invocation pipes remote script content directly into bash with no local review or pinning; mirrored as irm|iex in install.ps1:4,7 and repeated at install.sh:8,430,468

evidence: install.sh:157-162 curl-downloads and tar-extracts the release asset with no checksum step; install.ps1:263-294 SHA256-verifies the same release asset against checksums.txt before installing
description: Unix installer path installs a downloaded release binary onto the system PATH with no integrity verification, unlike the PowerShell sibling installer
suggested_fix: Add SHA256 checksum verification against the release's checksums.txt before moving the binary into place, mirroring install.ps1

evidence: install.sh:169-170: sudo mv "$tmp_dir/multica" "$bin_dir/multica" when bin_dir is not writable
description: Installer escalates to sudo to move the downloaded binary into /usr/local/bin when not writable

evidence: install.sh:178-179 exports PATH for the current shell; add_to_path() at 187-195 appends export PATH=... to ~/.bashrc and ~/.zshrc
description: Installer modifies the user's PATH for the current session and persists the change into shell rc files

evidence: apps/desktop/package.json:33 postinstall: electron-builder install-app-deps; apps/web/package.json:13 and apps/docs/package.json:12 postinstall: fumadocs-mdx
description: Three package manifests declare postinstall scripts; all invoke well-known, actively maintained build tooling with no suspicious arguments or network calls
Skill fetches guidelines from a mutable main branch ref via WebFetch and lets the fetched content dictate its own output format instructions at run time
suggested_fix: Pin the fetch URL to a specific commit SHA or tagged release and re-pin deliberately on upstream updates

description: Multiple scripts (check.sh, dev.sh, install.sh) make outbound curl calls for localhost health checks and GitHub release lookups, plus docker compose pull

evidence: package.json:27 "ui:add": "cd packages/ui && npx shadcn@latest add" uses @latest while every other dependency in the file is version-ranged or catalog:-pinned
description: ui:add script installs shadcn@latest unpinned, unlike the rest of the manifest's pinned/ranged dependencies
suggested_fix: Pin shadcn to a specific version or document that this interactive dev command intentionally tracks latest

evidence: argument-hint: <file-or-pattern> is nested under metadata: (frontmatter lines 4-7) instead of being a top-level key as documented in skills/nlpm/conventions-claude
description: argument-hint is a reserved top-level Claude Code field but is nested inside a custom metadata: block, so /help will not display the intended argument hint for this skill
suggested_fix: Move argument-hint to the frontmatter root; keep author/version under metadata: if they are meant as free-form vendor metadata

description: allowed-tools uses comma-separated scalar tool patterns (Bash(multica *), Bash(git *), Bash(gh *)) while skills/nlpm/conventions-claude documents the scalar-string form as space-separated
suggested_fix: Use a space-separated scalar ("Bash(multica *) Bash(git *) Bash(gh *)") or switch to a YAML list form

description: Skill's core behavior depends on a live WebFetch of third-party content with no documented fallback if the fetch fails or returns unexpected content
suggested_fix: Document a fallback (e.g. use last-known-good cached rules, or fail loudly) if the WebFetch call errors

evidence: all 8 references/*-source-map.md files confirmed present via Glob for multica-autopilots, multica-creating-agents, multica-mentioning, multica-projects-and-resources, multica-runtimes-and-repos, multica-skill-importing, multica-squads, multica-working-on-issues
description: All references/*-source-map.md sidecars cited by the 8 builtin_skills SKILL.md files exist on disk; no broken references found
